free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of 240-MeV 6Li ions
from 40Ca and and tests of a systematic optical
potential
Experiment performed at the Texas A&M
K500 superconducting cyclotron
Multipole-dipole-multipole (MDM) spectrometer
was used to analyze reaction products. The horizontal
acceptance of the spectrometer was 4 deg.
Focal plane detector consisted of four
resistive wire proportional couters to
measure position.
Particle in the focal plane were
identified with a telescope consisting of ionization
chamber to measure dE and a scintillator to measure E.
Position resolution of 0.9  mm and scattering angle
resolution of 0.09deg was achieved.
Total uncertainty containing systematic
uncertainties in target thickness, solid angle, flux
determination and dead time correction
Data corrected for dead time
Compiled by S.H.
Self-supporting 40Ca target foil, enriched to more
than 95% in 40Ca with thickness of 5.02 mg/cm2.
Data presented in fig. 1 of the reference sent
by author (Krishichayan)
